title: Python Basics Exam
description: Check your understanding of Python fundamentals used in AI workflows, including data types, control flow, functions, and NumPy basics.

questions.question: Which Python data structure is best suited for storing model hyperparameters as key-value pairs?
questions.explanation: A dictionary maps keys to values, making it a natural fit for configuration objects such as learning rates, batch sizes, and optimizer names.

questions.question: What does NumPy vectorization usually help you avoid?

options.label: Writing explicit Python loops for element-wise numeric operations
Using arrays to store numeric data

questions.explanation: Vectorized NumPy operations run optimized array code under the hood, so you can express numerical transformations without manual Python loops.

questions.question: A Python tuple is mutable after creation.
questions.explanation: Tuples are immutable. They are commonly used for fixed values such as input shapes or coordinate pairs.

questions.question: Which steps are commonly part of a basic AI data preprocessing pipeline?

options.label: Split data into train and test sets
Delete the target variable before training

questions.explanation: A typical preprocessing flow cleans missing values, scales features when needed, and splits data for evaluation. The target variable must be separated, not deleted entirely.

questions.question: What is the output shape when a feature matrix with shape (3, 2) is multiplied by a weight matrix with shape (2, 4)?
questions.explanation: Matrix multiplication keeps the outer dimensions. A (3, 2) matrix multiplied by a (2, 4) matrix produces a (3, 4) result.
